channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: How to Measure Token Impact of MCP Tool Invocation in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Your MCP-enabled agent just ran, and the token counts don't add up. The API says 773 tokens. The portal trace shows 581/141. The trajectory view displays something else entirely. Before you file a bug report, here's what's actually happening—and how to build an evidence pattern that makes...
item.dc:creator: vrajakishore

item.title: ArchAngel: Skilling the next developer generation for the Agentic transformation.
item.description: AI is transforming the SDLC at speed but there's a quieter question following close behind. If the code is being written for your junior developers, when do they learn the skills to become senior ones and how can they use these tools without losing understanding of what they've made? Most tools try...
item.dc:creator: RohitMadhavKrishnan

item.title: Troubleshooting ML Model Loading, GPU Issues, and Memory Pressure in Azure Container Apps
item.description: Introduction Deploying an AI application to Azure Container Apps is fundamentally different from deploying a web API. When you containerize a Django REST API, the application starts in a few seconds, the memory footprint is predictable, and the CPU usage scales linearly with requests. When you...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: Orchestration Patterns for Multi-Agent Systems: Performance and Trade-offs
item.description: Real-world lessons from evolving a production chatbot into a coordinator-based multi-agent architecture, including performance trade-offs for enterprise-scale agent reuse. The post Orchestration Patterns for Multi-Agent Systems: Performance and Trade-offs appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lily Jia

item.title: June Patches for Azure DevOps Server
item.description: We are releasing new patches for our self‑hosted product, Azure DevOps Server. We strongly recommend that all customers stay up to date with the latest, most secure version of Azure DevOps Server. The most recent release, Azure DevOps Server, is available on the download page. The following...
item.dc:creator: Gloridel Morales

item.title: Right‑sizing Azure Savings Plans, one hour at a time
item.description: Most of us have, at some point, sized a commitment the same way we order food for a team offsite: estimate, round up, and hope nobody complains. It works for pizza. It is a less robust strategy for a three‑year Savings Plan. Azure Cost Management already produces commitment recommendations for you,...
item.dc:creator: Dirk_Brinkmann

item.title: A deep dive into Azure Bastion session recording
item.description: If you’ve worked with Azure Bastion’s Premium SKU, you’ve likely encountered one of its most powerful security features: graphical session recording. Capturing RDP and SSH sessions end to end strengthens compliance, supports forensic investigations, and improves operational accountability. How you...
item.dc:creator: juquint

item.title: Modern VM monitoring, powered by OpenTelemetry
item.description: At Build 2026, we're announcing the general availability of OpenTelemetry (OTel) Guest OS metrics for Azure VMs and Arc-enabled Servers. OTel provides a standards-based foundation for VM monitoring with consistent metrics across Windows and Linux, richer Guest OS and per-process visibility, and...
item.dc:creator: viviandiec

item.title: What to Do When You Hit Capacity in Azure Databricks: Engage, Mitigate, Plan!
item.description: Microsoft's Cloud Architects: Paul Singh PaulSingh​, Eduardo Dos Santos eduardomdossantos​, Chris Walk cwalk​, Peter Lo PeterLo​, Tim Orentlikher tim_orentlikher​, Ajmal Hossain ajmalhossain​, Chris Haynes Chris_Haynes​, and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Start Here: Engage Microsoft Capacity constraints...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Transitioning from Azure Language Features to Foundry Models
item.description: Azure AI Language in Foundry Tools will begin deprecating eight features: Key Phrase Extraction, Entity Linking, Sentiment Analysis & Opinion Mining, Summarization (document & conversation), Conversational Language Understanding (CLU), Custom Question Answering (CQA), Orchestration Workflow, and...
item.dc:creator: renaliu

item.title: Azure Monitor SLIs now Generally Available
item.description: Azure Monitor SLIs are now generally available Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs) in Azure Monitor are now generally available. Teams can now measure reliability based on customer experience, not just infrastructure signals. SLI: A quantitative measure of how well...
item.dc:creator: Sokuma

item.title: Azure Monitor Metrics Export Generally Available
item.description: Today, we’re excited to announce the general availability of Azure Monitor Metrics Export using data collection rules (DCRs). A scalable, flexible way to continuously export platform metrics with dimensional fidelity, lower latency, and more control over what you send downstream. Azure Monitor...
item.dc:creator: Sokuma

item.title: Copilot Autofix for GitHub Advanced Security for Azure DevOps
item.description: Over the last few years, we’ve encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub, where the newest AI-powered and agentic development experiences land first. Migrating isn’t equally simple for everyone. A move to GitHub can range from straightforward to a multi-year...
item.dc:creator: Laura Jiang

item.title: Azure Functions at Build 2026 Update
item.description: Azure Functions took another big leap at Build 2026. It is now the best programming model for event-driven apps and agents, on the best infrastructure to write secure code that scales. The headline features: serverless agents, connectors to M365, Teams, and more, Go, MCP, and Durable Tasks....
item.dc:creator: nzthiago

item.title: Review pull requests without leaving Visual Studio
item.description: Pull request integration in Visual Studio has been one of the most requested Git features. Developers have been asking for a way to open a PR, inspect the changes, discuss feedback, and finish the review without switching to the browser. The feedback on that request has played a big role in shaping...
item.dc:creator: Leah Tran

item.title: Real-Time Simulation with Ansys Discovery on Azure AMD Radeon V710 GPUs
item.description: Overview of Synopsys-Ansys Discovery Ansys Discovery is an interactive engineering application that unifies 3D geometry modeling, real‑time simulation, and visual analysis into a single, immersive workspace. It is purpose‑built to accelerate early‑stage engineering exploration while maintaining...
item.dc:creator: Sunita_AZ0708

item.title: Microsoft Fabric to Azure Databricks: Another Better Together Story!
item.description: Author's: Oscar Alvarado oscaralvarado​ and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Note: This article describes a solution idea. Your cloud architect can use this guidance to help visualize the major components for a typical implementation. Use this article as a starting point to design a well-architected solution...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Dynamic Connection Properties in Azure Logic Apps Standard
item.description: Switching Built-in Connections at Runtime The Problem Imagine your organization has a single Logic App workflow used by multiple teams. Each team has its own SFTP server, database, or service bus namespace. Traditionally, you'd need separate workflows or hardcode a single connection — neither...
item.dc:creator: ShubhamSachdeva99

item.title: Debug App Startup Faster on Azure App Service for Linux with Startup Logs
item.description: When an app fails to start on Azure App Service for Linux, one of the first things you need is visibility into what happened during startup. This can include container initialization, runtime setup, startup command execution, application output, and warmup probe results. To make this easier, we...
item.dc:creator: TulikaC

item.title: Analyse Intune Diagnostics Logs with GitHub Copilot
item.description: Hello everyone! I’m Stefan Röll, Cloud Solution Architect at Microsoft Germany for Cloud Endpoints. I work closely with IT Admins to improve their experience with everything around Intune, W365 and Windows. I know that troubleshooting an issue can be time consuming and challenging. In this blog I...
item.dc:creator: StefanRöll

item.title: Performance Tuning Cold Starts, Scaling Delays, and Startup Latency in Azure Container Apps
item.description: Introduction There is a particular kind of frustration that comes not when your application fails to start, but when it starts too slowly. The container is running, the health probes pass, your monitoring shows green — and yet every few minutes a user somewhere in the world hits a request that...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: At-Scale Failure Reporting for Azure Update Manager
item.description: Introduction Azure Update Manager simplifies patching across Azure virtual machines and Azure Arc-enabled servers by providing a centralized platform for patch assessment and installation. However, as environments scale, a key challenge emerges—efficiently identifying and troubleshooting patch...
item.dc:creator: rajeshkumar30

item.title: Now in Foundry: Command A+ (W4A4), Chandra OCR 2, and GLM-OCR
item.description: We are seeing two distinct trends this week. The first is around how low-bit quantization has developed to the point where large reasoning models can fit on a single accelerator with less quality loss. Second, a new wave of OCR-specialized vision-language models are redefining the...
item.dc:creator: Osi

item.title: Announcing Azure Linux 4.0: Purpose-Built for Azure, Now in Public Preview
item.description: Today at Microsoft Build, we're announcing the public preview of Azure Linux 4.0 - Microsoft's first party Linux distribution, purpose-built for Azure. Azure Linux 4.0 is available now for Azure Virtual Machines, VM Scale Sets, and container images – with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) support and...
item.dc:creator: poorvinarang

item.title: Deploying Foundry Hosted Agents from Source Code
item.description: Introduction At Microsoft Build, it was announced that Foundry Hosted Agents now support source-code deployments. Previously, Hosted Agents required application code to be packaged in a container for deployment. This new functionality allows you to deploy the agent from a `.zip` file instead of...
item.dc:creator: j_folberth

item.title: Introducing the New Browser Automation Tool with Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description: Your AI agent just analyzed 500 invoices—but it can't click the 'Submit' button to process them. AI agents can reason through complex problems, but they hit a wall when workflows require interacting with web interfaces The Browser Automation Tool bridges that gap, giving your agents the ability to...
item.dc:creator: NandiniMuralidharan

item.title: Connecting Claude Clients with Azure API Management and Claude Models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Summary You want to give your engineering org Claude Code without handing out Anthropic API keys, without per-developer billing sprawl, and without losing visibility into who is spending what. This post shows a battle-tested pattern: Claude models run in Microsoft Foundry, billed through your Azure...
item.dc:creator: MuraliKumanduri

item.title: PUBLIC PREVIEW - Azure Monitor - Collect Azure Resource Platform Logs at Scale with DCRs
item.description: The Challenge of Platform Log Collection at Scale For many platform teams, Azure resource platform log collection starts simple—but becomes difficult to manage at enterprise scale. Imagine a team responsible for more than 8,000 Azure resources across 14 subscriptions. Every new resource needs a...
item.dc:creator: Mahesh_Sundaram

item.title: Announcing the Path to Production for Agents Webinar Series
item.description: Many organizations have made significant progress exploring AI—building pilots, prototypes, and proofs of concept. Yet a common challenge remains: how do you move from promising experiments to production-ready systems that are secure, scalable, and trusted? Join us for the Path to Production...
item.dc:creator: brauerblogs

item.title: We Gave Ourselves 20 Minutes to Build an AI Agent for a Lumber Company. The Timer's Still on Screen.
item.description: Here's a confession: most "build with AI" webinars are 60 minutes of slides, 5 minutes of a polished demo someone rehearsed for a week, and a closing CTA. You leave inspired but not really sure what you saw. So we tried something different. We put a visible countdown timer on the screen and gave...
item.dc:creator: JoshuaHuang

item.title: PowerToys 0.100 is here: new Shortcut Guide, Command Palette improvements and much more!
item.description: We’re back with a fresh PowerToys release! This month introduces the brand-new Shortcut Guide, a major Command Palette update with the new Extension Gallery and multi-monitor Dock support, and a wave of improvements to Power Display. We’ve also upgraded PowerToys to .NET 10, improved auto-update...
item.dc:creator: Niels Laute

item.title: Productize, observe, version, and automate MCP servers in Azure API Management
item.description: Introduction As organizations move from AI-assisted applications to agentic workflows, MCP servers are becoming a critical integration layer between agents, tools, APIs, data sources, and enterprise systems. Azure API Management already helps teams bring MCP servers under enterprise governance. But...
item.dc:creator: anishta

item.title: How ARM Tracks Work That Takes Hours
item.description: By Arav Goyal, Joy Shah, Michael Cheng, Manik Sikka, Jenny Hunter, Johnson Shi Introduction When a user creates, updates, or deletes a resource in Azure, the request flows through Azure Resource Manager (ARM) before reaching the service that actually owns the resource. For operations that complete...
item.dc:creator: aravgoyal

item.title: ACI Sandboxes: A Glimpse into the Future of Agentic Compute
item.description: Emerging Technology Preview We are delighted to share innovation in the world of AI. At Build, we demonstrated several innovations that showcase where Azure Container Instances (ACI) is heading and how it can become a foundational platform for the next generation of AI workloads. As AI agents...
item.dc:creator: DanaCozmei

item.title: Ginkgo Bioworks and Microsoft Discovery: Bringing agentic AI to biological discovery
item.description: Introduction Biological discovery is inherently iterative and non-linear. Progress comes through cycles of hypothesis, experimentation, refinement, and review across data, tools, and teams. At Microsoft Build 2026, Microsoft announced general availability of Microsoft Discovery to support exactly...
item.dc:creator: NihitPokhrel

item.title: Online Meeting Effectiveness Index (OMEI)
item.description: Samidha Shikhare*, Tim Malueg*, Shreya Goel*, and Charlie Goldenberg* Department of Business Analytics, Santa Clara University - Leavey School of Business, Santa Clara, California 95053, United States *These authors contributed equally. {sshikhare, tmalueg, sgoel, cgoldenberg}@scu.edu This research...
item.dc:creator: juhisingh

item.title: Distributed multi-agent systems with Aspire and Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: Learn how Aspire, Microsoft Agent Framework, and Microsoft Foundry model, run, observe, and publish a distributed multi-agent AlpineAI ski resort demo. The post Distributed multi-agent systems with Aspire and Microsoft Agent Framework appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Tommaso Stocchi

item.title: From AI Suggestions to Autonomous CRM Actions in Dynamics 365
item.description: 🔷 Executive Summary Most AI implementations in Dynamics 365 start—and end—with case summarization. While useful, summarization alone does not fundamentally transform service operations. In this post, I’ll walk through a CRM Copilot Agent Accelerator built on Microsoft Power Platform that goes far...
item.dc:creator: SachinDas

item.title: Copilot Code Reviews for Azure Repos
item.description: Over the last several years, we have encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub to take advantage of the latest AI powered and agentic development experiences that GitHub is delivering. At the same time, we recognize that migrating repositories to GitHub can vary...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem, Andrew Brenner

item.title: Troubleshooting Azure Container Apps and Jobs for .NET and Django Workloads
item.description: Introduction Deploying to Azure Container Apps feels like a huge step forward — you get serverless containers, automatic scaling, built-in ingress, and managed environments without managing Kubernetes directly. But when something goes wrong and your container refuses to start, or your Container App...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: Any source. Any destination. Ready for AI-era.
item.description: Telemetry is exploding, every new app, edge node, and AI agent is a new firehose, and AI has raised the bar on what that telemetry must be: governed, on open standards, observable at agent scale. Today, most teams answer that by stitching together a stack of disconnected tools, each catering to a...
item.dc:creator: shayoni_seth

item.title: What's new in Azure App Service at #MSBuild 2026
item.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, Azure App Service introduced a powerful set of updates designed to help organizations accelerate their journey into AI, without increasing complexity or cost. These innovations focus on one clear business outcome: enabling teams to build, deploy, and scale AI-powered...
item.dc:creator: Mayunk_Jain

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026 recap: vision, launches, and top sessions
item.description: Catch up on Microsoft Build 2026 with the vision lead-off, top developer announcements, and must-watch sessions across the Microsoft developer ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Jon Galloway

item.title: Write Logic Apps in C#: introducing the Logic Apps Standard SDK
item.description: The workflow you always wished you could write in code If you build on Logic Apps Standard, you already know the deal: the runtime is excellent at the unglamorous parts of integration - connecting to systems, retrying, scaling, keeping run history you can actually debug. What you sometimes wanted...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Logic Apps Aviators Newsletter - June 2026
item.description: In this issue: Ace Aviator of the Month News from our product group News from our community Ace Aviator of the Month June 2026's Ace Aviator: Florian De Langhe LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/floriandelanghe/ Florian De Langhe Lead Expert/Team Lead - Microsoft Integration @ delaware What's...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Designing for High Availability: The Operational Reference for Running a Geo-Replicated ACR
item.description: By Johnson Shi, Zoey (Zhuyu) Li, Huangli Wu Introduction Three of the most common questions we hear from enterprise teams running geo-replicated Azure Container Registries (ACR) are: "How do I control which region serves my traffic?" — When my AKS clusters are spread across regions, can I pin each...
item.dc:creator: johnsonshi_msft

item.title: Connect Metrics to Traces with Exemplars in Azure Monitor
item.description: Following Microsoft’s recent GA announcement for OpenTelemetry (OTel) support, we are excited to announce support for Exemplars for customers instrumenting metrics with Prometheus or OpenTelemetry and traces using OpenTelemetry, enhancing Azure Monitor’s integrated observability experience for...
item.dc:creator: sunayanasingh

item.title: Driving Stop-and-Go Business Processes to Closure with Foundry Hosted Agents
item.description: Why This Matters Your AI agent just forgot everything. Again. If you've built agents that need to track work across days or weeks—not just single conversations—you've hit this wall: the agent loop is stateless. Each turn reasons over what it's handed, then forgets. But real business workflows don't...
item.dc:creator: srikantan

item.title: Enterprise Live Migrations: Moving from Azure DevOps Repo to GitHub with minimal disruption
item.description: Over the last several years, we’ve encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub to take advantage of the latest AI-powered and agentic development experiences. For many enterprise teams, however, migrating at scale comes with real constraints. Traditional approaches...
item.dc:creator: Soo Stahl, Bhuvan Shah

item.title: New MAI models in Microsoft Foundry across text, image, voice, and speech
item.description: Continuing Microsoft AI momentum in Foundry Since launching MAI-Image-2-Efficient, MAI-Image-2, MAI-Voice-1, MAI-Transcribe-1 in Microsoft Foundry this spring, we've been laser-focused on one thing: giving developers the most complete first-party AI stack to build with. Today, at Microsoft Build...
item.dc:creator: Naomi Moneypenny
